beat.title: Waking in the Cell
beat.boundaryReason: The session opens with the four prisoners waking in a crude wooden cell, introducing themselves, finding the rotten plank escape, scavenging tools and clothes from the adjoining rooms, overhearing the drunk goblins beyond the door, and finally settling on a plan to rush the guards from two sides.
dateEvidence: the DM says it is 'just past noon' while describing the prison room lighting
nothing in this opening escape sequence suggests a rest, sunset, or date transition before the party commits to the ambush

sourceLines.text: We will start our game tonight in a dimly lit room.

beat.title: The Drunk Goblins Fall
beat.boundaryReason: Once the ambush starts, the whole scene stays focused on the first guard-room fight: the party fumbles the doorway plan, rolls into initiative, and then steadily cuts down the three drunk goblin jailers in the forge-side room before the magical darkness and the fight both end.
dateEvidence: this beat begins immediately after the group finalizes the ambush and opens the fight
the transcript frames the guards as already drunk in early afternoon, with no rest or scene reset before combat
the beat ends as the darkness effect drops and the first fight is fully resolved on the same day

beat.title: Breather on the Manor Grounds
beat.boundaryReason: With the guard room secured, the party loots weapons and stolen gear, steps outside to understand the ruined manor grounds around them, recovers equipment and food, takes a short rest and level-up pause, and then surveys the nearby barracks and overgrown estate while deciding what kind of approach to try next.
dateEvidence: the beat starts directly after the first combat ends, with no overnight break
the DM says the party is outside in 'mid-afternoon, maybe 2 or 3' while describing the manor grounds
the short rest and subsequent planning all occur in that same daylight stretch without any sunset or next-day cue

beat.title: A Goblin Ruse Unravels
beat.boundaryReason: The next phase is entirely about the deception attempt: the party decides to use Loria's Goblin and silent speech with Gaudin's disguise, stages a fake prisoner transfer outside the barracks, and almost sells the act before Folcan and the other goblins grow suspicious and Loria breaks the standoff with a fog cloud.
dateEvidence: this beat follows the manor-ground planning immediately, with no intervening rest
the DM explicitly calls it late afternoon while the disguised approach plays out near the barracks
the whole exchange is one continuous social gambit that does not advance the date before open violence starts

beat.title: Fog Fight by the Barracks
beat.boundaryReason: Once the fog cloud goes up, the deception is over and the whole beat becomes a tight skirmish outside the barracks, with the party using the hedgerow and obscuring mist to kill the goblin guards quickly enough to avoid drawing the full barracks, then dragging the surviving boss back for questioning.
dateEvidence: the beat begins at the moment the failed ruse converts into open initiative
the DM warns that the barracks will hear the fight after only a few rounds, underscoring that the whole battle is a brief continuous exchange
the scene ends immediately after the last foe is subdued and the party withdraws with their captive on the same afternoon

beat.title: Folcan Names the Tunnels
beat.boundaryReason: Back at the forge and hedge line, the party spends the rest of the session interrogating Folcan, learning how Grimstone's prisoner racket works, how many goblins and hobgoblins are nearby, and where the tunnel entrances might reach the wine cellar, before ending on a tentative plan to send Folcan back and exploit the tunnels next time.
dateEvidence: the transcript gives the party 'about 50 min to interrogate this guy' immediately after the second fight
the follow-up discussion reads as one uninterrupted aftermath scene rather than a new day or night watch
the session ends in planning conversation with no evidence of a sleep, dawn, or date rollover beyond the same afternoon
